steps.title: Identify similar triangles using angle relationships
steps.content:
Since $\overline{LQ}$ passes through $R$, and $\overline{MP}$ passes through $R$, we have two lines intersecting at $R$. This means $\angle LRM$ and $\angle PRQ$ are vertical angles, so $\angle LRM = \angle PRQ$.

Since $\overline{LM} \parallel \overline{PQ}$ and $\overline{LQ}$ is a transversal cutting both lines, $\angle MLR = \angle PQR$ are alternate interior angles.

By AA similarity, $\triangle LMR \sim \triangle PQR$.

steps.title: Find the linear similarity ratio
steps.content: The sides $MR$ and $PR$ are corresponding sides in the similar triangles. The ratio is: $$\text{Similarity ratio} = \frac{MR}{PR} = \frac{8}{14} = \frac{4}{7}$$

steps.title: Apply the area scaling formula and solve
steps.content:
For similar figures, areas scale by the square of the linear scale factor: $$\frac{\text{Area}(\triangle LMR)}{\text{Area}(\triangle PQR)} = \left(\frac{4}{7}\right)^2 = \frac{16}{49}$$
Given $\text{Area}(\triangle LMR) = 36$: $$\text{Area}(\triangle PQR) = 36 \times \frac{49}{16} = \frac{36 \times 49}{16} = \frac{1764}{16} = \frac{441}{4}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
